text: In 2017, a leaked internal Facebook document revealed that the platform had offered advertisers the ability to target teenagers in moments of emotional vulnerability — sadness, insecurity, the need for confidence. This was not a rogue experiment but the logical endpoint of behavioral targeting: if attention is currency, then attention seized from a person at their weakest moment is simply the highest-yield transaction. The document exposed what the attention economy requires but rarely states plainly — that profit maximization and user wellbeing are not complementary goals but structurally competing ones.
question: What did the leaked Facebook document reveal about the logic of behavioral targeting?

options.text: Targeting teenagers in vulnerable moments was an accidental feature of the algorithm
Profit maximization and user wellbeing are complementary when advertising is personalized
Advertising worked best when shown to users who were emotionally resilient
Attention seized during moments of vulnerability represented the highest-yield transactions
